name: Y O
text: The first floor is really like a farmers' market. Full of laughing, loud talking and screaming. lol
responseFromOwnerText: We're glad you can laugh about this! With two entrances, a café, a service desk, exhibit spaces, walk-through traffic, student tour groups, and wonderful acoustics, the first floor is a lively space! Fortunately, the library offers several options for those who want to avoid ambient noise: the fourth floor is the “Quiet Zone” and successfully peer-maintained; the second floor study space is well used for concentrated work; the lower/basement level is also quiet and ringed by onyen-authenticated computers; and the third floor carrels (on the outer walls) provide quiet distance from the computer labs on that floor. Hopefully, there's a right place for everyone!
address: 335 S Columbia St, Chapel Hill, NC 27599
title: Health Sciences Library

name: Eddie Liu
text:
Eddie's Bathroom Reviews

Cleanliness: 5/5. Cleaned every day.
Toilet paper: 2/5. One ply.
Automation: 0/5. Everything is manual - toilet, sink, paper towel dispenser.
Sound level: 1/5. Minimal white noise
Decorations: 0/5. Nothing on the walls
Availability: 5/5. One of each gender on each floor

Overall: 4/5. Decent experience!
address: 335 S Columbia St, Chapel Hill, NC 27599
title: Health Sciences Library

name: Michael Duda
text: A really beautiful place to study if you are in the medical sciences.  Lots of open study space for both solo and group study.  Of course it is also a modern library with any type of medical text you could wish for.
